project: Bellwood Hub — Mayor's AI Chief of Staff
tagline: Graph-augmented, entity-resolved institutional-memory platform over the Village of Bellwood's ~70,000 municipal emails — a queryable chief of staff, not just search.
headline: 🟢 Live & deployed — the full Chief-of-Staff demo is on Vercel at web-seven-tawny-20.vercel.app (public, keyless, 30k-email seed) plus an Admin console, themes, and voice search. No blockers for Monday.

glance.d: The Mayor's landing screen: a 'Good morning, Mayor Harvey' Chief-of-Staff briefing (what happened / new / important) + today's calendar + sign-to-approve agent drafts inline + inbox preview, on mobile + desktop. A new R4 Chief of Staff agent summarizes every other agent's output. Hybrid generation: deterministic baseline keyless, OpenAI-voiced persona when a key is present; persona (name/greeting/tone/instructions) is configurable in Admin → Chief of Staff. Next: the Morning Agent (configurable web-search query cards, Supabase-backed).

Runs on JSON fixtures derived from the 30k seed — no DB or API dependency for the screens (DEMO_MODE=1). The bulletproof path for a live mayor demo; Ask still calls OpenAI for synthesis, and email drill-down hits Postgres.
Deployed to Vercel (public) and running locally. / redirects to /chief. Desktop ChiefApp + a dedicated mobile UI (bottom nav) switch at 768px. Public URL: web-seven-tawny-20.vercel.app.
Postgres (poc.*) fully loaded with OpenAI embeddings. Powers the click-through-to-source-email drill-down and the optional live semantic path (flip DEMO_MODE=0).
86,744 address · 43,370 person · 19,052 phone · 1,943 business rows across the corpus — the basis for the History (entity timeline) screen.

Project wwqebbqbnetkjibqmhlj (us-west-2, PG17). 19 tables across poc / canonical / pipeline / app schemas. Isolated from other projects for client-data separation; connects via the IPv4 session pooler.

Verified against the CTO brief: AD-1 ingestion decoupled from capability, AD-2 one Postgres / three access patterns, AD-3 resolved-conversation unit, AD-4 topics + RRF planner, AD-5 event-sourced issues, AD-6 assertion-ledger identity.

corpus.d: The institutional-memory asset — what the village actually buys. Matches the design prototype's 70,431 and the brief's ~70k reference corpus.
The seed corpus with full content (corpus/seed_emails.json) — what the demo runs on, loaded into Postgres with embeddings + entities.
Emails with no seed content yet — the heart of the pitch: deleted mail is dead storage that walks out at every election; recovering it into queryable memory is the product's reason to exist.

rows.t: TASK-1 · Capture Outlook data for the Mayor's mailbox (Exchange/Graph)
rows.detail: Build a real Outlook/Exchange connector via Microsoft Graph API (Mail.Read) to ingest the Mayor's actual mailbox into the pipeline — implementing the 5-step contract in ingest/base.py (pull → normalize → resolve → classify → index+emit), replacing synthetic_email.py. Captures inbox + recovered/deleted items toward the ~70k corpus. Needs Azure app registration + admin consent. Concrete version of product task #3. Outlook is also the source for the Calendar (TASK-11).

rows.t: TASK-11 · Calendar view from MS Outlook calendar
rows.detail: The Calendar screen (renamed from Events) should be a true calendar view loading from the Mayor's MS Outlook calendar via Microsoft Graph (Calendars.Read) — events, times, locations, attendees — with a day/week/agenda UI, reconciled with the email-derived action items. Pairs with TASK-1 (Outlook is the source for both mail and calendar).

rows.t: TASK-2 · Ship search index to Vercel (BUG-1)
rows.detail: Resolved via next.config outputFileTracingIncludes (no trimming needed) — the 31 MB index ships with the /api/ask function and compresses to ~6 MB, within limits. Verified: live Ask returns the curated Gloria Bennett answer with sources.

rows.t: TASK-3 · Deploy the app to Vercel (resilient URL)
rows.detail: Deployed. Public URL: https://web-seven-tawny-20.vercel.app (also bellwoodhub.vercel.app, gated by Deployment Protection). Runs in keyless demo mode (no DATABASE_URL); OpenAI key set for live Ask + voice. American-flag favicon shipped. Mitigates RSK-2.
Flip DEMO_MODE=0 in a throwaway env and verify semantic Ask, Memory drill-ins, Sources, and Brief against Postgres. Acceptance: parity with the JSON demo, no 500s. Mitigates RSK-3.

rows.t: TASK-5 · Wire Events to seed data
rows.detail: DONE. Built an events extractor (web/scripts/build-demo.mjs) that folds actionable corpus threads into events (responded→done, stale inbound→overdue, recent→open) → events.json + /api/events. Mobile + desktop Events screens render 40 real items with drill-to-source. Closes ISS-1.

rows.t: TASK-6 · Clean Brief's static hero cards
rows.detail: Replace the Brief screen's hardcoded BriefCards (Water-main bid, etc.) with seed-derived items. Acceptance: nothing on the Brief is invented. Closes ISS-2.

rows.t: TASK-7 · Rotate API keys after demo
rows.detail: Rotate OpenAI, Voyage, Supabase service-role, and the DB password (all shared in plaintext in chat). Move to Vercel env / a secret manager. Acceptance: old keys revoked, app still works. Mitigates RSK-1.

80% of usage is mobile but /chief is desktop-first (fixed inline-styled sidebar, multi-column grids). The new theme switcher makes all schemes mobile-legible and a CSS pass scales padding/fonts, but the layout needs a real refactor: collapse the sidebar to a bottom tab bar / hamburger and single-column the workspaces on narrow screens.

Iterate on the color-scheme system just shipped (Midnight / Dim / Daylight / High-contrast + top-bar toggle + Admin → Appearance). Likely scope: add/refine palette options (more schemes or an accent-colour picker), polish the Daylight theme where glassy overlays read flat, verify WCAG-AA contrast across all schemes, and confirm mobile legibility. Awaiting RD's specific direction on which options to add/change.

rows.t: TASK-10 · Security audit before going live
rows.detail: Full pre-production security audit / penetration test before any real mailbox data goes live — a go-live gate. Scope: adversarial threat model (brief §9 — a sitting official's email: FOIA, opposition research, breach exposure); enforce row-level RBAC via Postgres RLS (currently stubbed — ISS-4); PII detection + tagging at ingestion; encryption in transit + at rest; least-privilege DB roles per pipeline stage; secrets management + key rotation (RSK-1 / TASK-7); the immutable audit trail (ISS-5); NIST 800-53 + CJIS §5.4 control review for the police CJI data; dependency/SCA scan; auth review. Engage municipal counsel on FOIA / Open Meetings / records-retention. Pairs with ISS-5 (audit) and ISS-4 (RLS) — all post-demo.

blocker.text: None for the Monday demo. It runs keyless on the JSON path. Full DB-backed semantic search is one env flip away (DEMO_MODE=0) — the database is loaded and ready; kept on JSON for reliability.

issues.title: No audit trail — must meet NIST 800-53 AU + CJIS §5.4 government auditing standards
issues.detail: ⏳ SCHEDULED POST-DEMO (RD, Jun 26) — deferred until after Monday; required before any real mailbox data. CRITICAL go-live blocker (not a demo blocker). STANDARDS: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 'AU' (Audit & Accountability) family is the federal baseline; CJIS Security Policy §5.4 applies because police blotter/incident data is Criminal Justice Information; add HIPAA §164.312(b) audit controls if fire/EMS PHI is indexed; retention per the Illinois Local Records Act. REQUIREMENTS — (1) LOG EVERY EVENT [AU-2/AU-12, CJIS 5.4.1.1]: searches/retrievals (/api/ask), record & document accesses (/api/email = CJI access), agent drafts, approvals/decisions, exports, logins (success+fail), permission and config changes. (2) RECORD CONTENT [AU-3, AU-8, CJIS 5.4.1.1.1]: synchronized timestamp, event type, system component, source (session/IP), actor identity, target (message_id/entity), outcome (success/fail), plus query text + result count. (3) PROTECTION [AU-9, AU-10]: append-only, tamper-evident (hash-chained), immutable — not modifiable or deletable by application roles; non-repudiation. (4) RETENTION [AU-11, CJIS]: at least 365 days, then per the local records schedule. (5) REVIEW & REPORTING [AU-6/AU-7, CJIS 5.4.3]: at-least-weekly review, searchable, exportable reports. IMPLEMENTATION: an append-only audit table + write-through middleware on every API action, written under a protected DB role, with a review/export surface.

issues.title: Events (was Commitments) screen on static content
issues.detail: CLOSED via TASK-5. The Events screen (mobile + desktop) now runs on real seed-derived data via /api/events — 40 actionable items (264 open / 656 overdue / 551 done) folded from the corpus threads, each clickable to its source email.

issues.title: search-index.json (31 MB) vs Vercel serverless bundle limit
issues.detail: CLOSED — solved via next.config outputFileTracingIncludes rather than trimming. The index ships with the Ask function (compresses to ~6 MB) and live Ask works on Vercel.

issues.title: Brief had hardcoded hero cards
issues.detail: CLOSED via TASK-6. Both the mobile Emails screen and the desktop Brief are now a clean, dense inbox list (Needs you / Inbox / Queued) on real seed data — the full inbox feed (/api/inbox, 21,169 inbound) plus needs-attention + queued drafts. No hardcoded cards. Scales to volume; every row drills to its source email.

issues.title: "Who emails me most" surfaces institutional senders (Cook County DOT) over residents
issues.detail: The aggregate ranks by raw inbound count, so institutions (Cook County DOT, IDOT, Metra) outrank residents. Consider biasing to external persons for a more constituent-centric demo.

issues.title: Supabase advisor: 9 tables have RLS disabled (anon-key exposure) — fine for synthetic, must enforce before real mailbox data
issues.detail: Supabase's advisor flags 9 RLS-disabled tables; the anon/publishable key could read them. Acceptable now (synthetic data, app uses the owner role which bypasses RLS anyway). Before real mailbox data: enable RLS + tenant policies + a non-bypass app role.

risks.title: API keys (OpenAI/Voyage/DB) were shared in plaintext in chat
risks.mitigation: Rotate all keys after the demo; move to Vercel env vars
risks.detail: OpenAI, Voyage, Supabase service-role, and the DB password were pasted into chat. They're gitignored locally, but the chat transcript retains them. Mitigation is TASK-7: rotate everything post-demo and move to managed env vars.

risks.title: ✓ MITIGATED — demo was local-only; now deployed to Vercel
risks.mitigation: Done — TASK-3 deployed the app to Vercel (public, resilient)
risks.detail: RESOLVED via TASK-3. The app is live and public at https://web-seven-tawny-20.vercel.app (also bellwoodhub.vercel.app once Deployment Protection is off), so a laptop sleep / network drop no longer kills the demo. Residual (low): keep the deployment warm and have the URL open as the primary; the local server is just a backup now.

risks.title: Live DB path (DEMO_MODE=0) not yet verified end-to-end
risks.mitigation: Verify semantic Ask + entity drill-ins before relying on it
risks.detail: The Postgres path is loaded (30k + embeddings + entities) but has never been exercised end-to-end through the UI. Mitigation is TASK-4: verify before any reliance or cutover.

risks.title: Embeddings use voyage-4-large but the brief specified voyage-3 (OQ-5 lock-in)
risks.mitigation: Confirm model + 1024-dim before any 70k backfill — re-embedding is the expensive replay path
risks.detail: The canonical store is vector(1024) using voyage-4-large; the architecture brief named voyage-3. The dimension matches, so vectors are usable, but the embedding model is a lock-in decision (OQ-5). Confirm before any full 70k backfill — re-embedding is the costly replay.

risks.title: Shared status page is public (no login)
risks.mitigation: Secrets-free now; lock behind Vercel auth before it carries anything sensitive
risks.detail: project-status-ten.vercel.app is public with no auth. Currently secrets-free and noindex, so fine. Before it ever carries client-sensitive detail, enable Vercel auth/SSO protection on that project.

risks.title: Agent anxiety — distrust of agents doing unapproved or automated work could block adoption
risks.detail: The biggest adoption risk: the Mayor and staff won't trust a system where agents act on their own. If trust isn't established, the product isn't used. MITIGATION: log every agent action — both human-approved and automated — to a visible, date-sorted Agent Activity page (FEAT-6) that links each action to the agent and its logs, backed by R3 human gates (agents draft, never send unprompted) and the immutable audit trail (ISS-5). Transparency is the trust mechanism.

deps.title: Deploy the Chief-of-Staff app itself to Vercel for a shareable mayor URL
deps.detail: RESOLVED — deployed and public at https://web-seven-tawny-20.vercel.app. Runs keyless demo mode; live Ask + voice via OpenAI key. The pretty alias bellwoodhub.vercel.app is set but gated by Deployment Protection (toggle off in project settings to make it public).

deps.title: BellwoodHub Postgres password (for the live DB path)
deps.detail: Provided. The live DB connects via the Supabase Session Pooler (aws-1-us-west-2:5432, IPv4) — the direct IPv6 host doesn't resolve on this network.

deps.title: API keys: OpenAI, Voyage, Supabase service-role
deps.detail: All provided and written to gitignored env files. OpenAI powers live Ask synthesis + Whisper voice; Voyage is for the canonical embedding path; service-role is stored for future REST use.

decisions.text: North Star: ONE place for the Mayor to look, search, and build his day. The Chief of Staff organizes his inbox, confirms his calendar (agent-supported), powers AI Search, and builds summaries by area. Unifying concept: configurable Area views — e.g., Firehouse (contacts · events · tasks), Police (emails · notices · tasks), Public Works — each a single domain workspace, built up over time (FEAT-3).
Direction: this becomes an agent that connects to other agents (agent-to-agent / MCP) — the capability agents + MCP server are the foundation.

Jobs-to-be-done IA: mobile reorganized around tasks — Emails (queued-to-go + needs-attention), Events (was Commitments), Search, History (was Memory), Source. Renamed throughout; kept "institutional memory" as the brand tagline. Every email is clickable to the full source document.
Kept the JSON demo as the default (DEMO_MODE=1) over the live DB — reliability for the mayor demo.
Connect via the Supabase Session Pooler (IPv4) instead of the direct IPv6 host — no IPv4 add-on needed.
Fixed a latent migration bug (canonical message_topics excluded from the RLS loop — no tenant_id column) so the schema applies cleanly on a fresh DB.
